cwe: CWE-770 - Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling

threat.title: Elasticsearch Expensive Query Denial of Service
threat.description: Elasticsearch clusters are vulnerable to resource exhaustion attacks through maliciously crafted queries that exploit wildcard searches on high-cardinality fields, deep aggregations, regex patterns, and script field execution. Attackers can cause heap memory exhaustion, CPU saturation, and cluster-wide outages by sending queries that pass circuit breaker pre-flight checks but expand exponentially during execution. Without proper query validation and resource limits, a single expensive query can crash data nodes and render the entire cluster unavailable.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies publicly accessible Elasticsearch cluster endpoint (e.g., port 9200)
2. Sends reconnaissance queries to identify high-cardinality fields (user_id, session_id, IP addresses)
3. Crafts malicious aggregation query targeting millions of unique buckets across nested fields
4. Query passes circuit breaker pre-flight estimation (appears safe)
5. During execution, query expands exponentially creating 50+ million buckets in memory
6. Data node JVM heap exhausts, triggering OutOfMemoryError and node crash
7. Cluster enters red state, production search requests fail, business operations halt
prerequisites: Elasticsearch cluster exposed to network (authenticated or unauthenticated)
No search.max_buckets limit configured (defaults to unlimited in older versions)
Circuit breakers not tuned or using default 60% request limit
No query timeout enforcement or request size validation
High-cardinality fields indexed without proper aggregation controls

cve_references.description: Elasticsearch Stack Overflow DoS vulnerability in _search API allowing specially crafted query strings to cause cluster crashes
Uncontrolled resource consumption via deeply nested pipeline processing on ingest nodes causing memory exhaustion

real_world_breaches.impact: Multiple cluster crashes per week over 2-week period; data nodes dying from JVM memory pressure spikes; production search degradation
real_world_breaches.root_cause: User-written aggregation queries creating millions of unique buckets without max_buckets limit; each bucket consumed heap memory causing exponential growth beyond circuit breaker estimates

real_world_breaches.impact: Cluster unavailability; heap memory exceeded 95% parent circuit breaker threshold; queries timing out across entire cluster
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Wildcard queries on high-cardinality fields combined with deep terms aggregations over 20+ million documents; inefficient query patterns causing 3-8 second response time degradation

real_world_breaches.impact: Complete search functionality outage during Black Friday peak traffic; estimated $2M+ revenue loss over 4-hour window
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Attacker exploited unprotected aggregation endpoint with scroll context leak; malicious regex queries with leading wildcards consumed 100% CPU on coordinating nodes

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Unauthenticated aggregation query causing cluster-wide OutOfMemoryError via cardinality explosion on _id field

security_research.title: Six Ways to Crash Elasticsearch
security_research.key_finding: Complex aggregation queries can bypass circuit breaker pre-flight checks and expand exponentially during execution, causing heap exhaustion even with default protections enabled

security_research.title: Elasticsearch and OpenSearch Query Limits Best Practices
security_research.key_finding: Search.max_buckets must be explicitly configured as older versions default to unlimited; aggregation memory consumption grows linearly with bucket count regardless of circuit breaker settings

mitigations.title: Configure Circuit Breakers and Resource Limits
mitigations.description: Implement strict circuit breaker thresholds and search.max_buckets limits to prevent individual queries from consuming excessive cluster resources. Circuit breakers trip before memory exhaustion, returning errors instead of crashing nodes.

code_example.code:
# elasticsearch.yml - Production Circuit Breaker Configuration

# REQUEST CIRCUIT BREAKER
# Limits memory for per-request data structures (aggregations, sorting)
indices.breaker.request.limit: 40%
indices.breaker.request.overhead: 2.0

# FIELDDATA CIRCUIT BREAKER
# Limits memory for loading field values into cache
indices.breaker.fielddata.limit: 35%
indices.breaker.fielddata.overhead: 1.03

# PARENT CIRCUIT BREAKER
# Overall JVM heap limit across all breakers
indices.breaker.total.limit: 70%

# IN-FLIGHT REQUESTS BREAKER
# Limits memory for all active incoming requests
network.breaker.inflight_requests.limit: 100%
network.breaker.inflight_requests.overhead: 2.0

# AGGREGATION BUCKET LIMIT
# Maximum buckets allowed in a single aggregation query
search.max_buckets: 10000

# QUERY TIMEOUTS
# Maximum time for search request execution
search.default_search_timeout: 30s
search.max_open_scroll_context: 500

# REJECT POLICY
# Return errors instead of crashing when limits exceeded
indices.breaker.total.use_real_memory: true

# THREAD POOL TUNING
# Prevent queue saturation from expensive queries
thread_pool.search.queue_size: 1000
thread_pool.search.size: 13  # (CPU cores / 2) + 1

# Validation:
# curl -X GET "localhost:9200/_cluster/settings?include_defaults=true&filter_path=*.*.breaker.*,*.*.search.*"

iso27001_controls: A.17.2.1 - Availability of information processing facilities
nist_csf: PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created and maintained
DE.AE-5 - Incident alert thresholds are established
validation_steps: Verify circuit breaker settings via GET /_cluster/settings API
Test aggregation query with >10,000 buckets (should fail with circuit_breaking_exception)
Monitor JVM heap pressure under load (should not exceed 70%)
Confirm request timeout enforcement after 30 seconds
Check thread pool rejection stats via GET /_cat/thread_pool

mitigations.title: Implement Query Validation and Sanitization Layer
mitigations.description: Add application-level query validation before forwarding to Elasticsearch. Reject queries with dangerous patterns (leading wildcards, unbounded regex, deep nesting) and enforce query complexity budgets.
nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
validation_steps: Test rejection of leading wildcard query: { wildcard: { field: '*value' } }
Verify >3 depth aggregation fails validation
Confirm script_fields queries are blocked
Test timeout enforcement with slow query simulation
Validate circuit_breaking_exception handling in application

mitigations.title: Deploy Rate Limiting and API Gateway Protection
mitigations.description: Implement rate limiting at the application and infrastructure layers to prevent query flooding attacks. Use API Gateway throttling policies and distributed rate limiters to cap requests per user/IP.
iso27001_controls: A.17.2.1 - Availability of information processing facilities
nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
DE.CM-7 - Monitoring for unauthorized activity is continuous
validation_steps: Test rate limit by sending >100 requests/minute (should return 429)
Verify Redis stores rate limit counters correctly
Confirm authenticated users have separate rate limits from IPs
Test aggregation endpoint allows max 10 requests per 5 minutes
Monitor rate limit metrics in application logs

mitigations.title: Enable Real-Time Query Performance Monitoring
mitigations.description: Deploy comprehensive monitoring for slow queries, circuit breaker trips, and heap pressure. Configure alerting for DoS indicators and automatic query termination for runaway searches.
nist_csf: DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect anomalies
RS.AN-1 - Notifications from detection systems are investigated
validation_steps: Trigger test circuit breaker exception and verify alert fires
Confirm slow query logs capture queries >2s
Test heap pressure alert at 85% threshold
Verify automatic task cancellation when heap exceeds limit
Check Prometheus metrics endpoint exposes breaker trip counts

indicators: Sudden spike in circuit_breaking_exception errors in Elasticsearch logs
JVM heap memory sustained above 85% for multiple nodes
Cluster health status transitions from green -> yellow -> red
Slow query logs show aggregation queries with >1M buckets
Thread pool rejection rate increases on search queue
Single IP address sending high volume of complex aggregation queries
Queries targeting high-cardinality fields (_id, session_id, IP addresses)
Scroll contexts accumulating without being cleared
CPU saturation (>90%) on data nodes coinciding with query spikes

log_queries.query:
// Detect Elasticsearch Circuit Breaker DoS Attacks
let lookbackWindow = 10m;
let circuitBreakerThreshold = 5;
AzureDiagnostics
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookbackWindow)
| where Category == "ElasticsearchLogs"
| where Message contains "circuit_breaking_exception" or Message contains "Data too large"
| extend SourceIP = extract(@"from\s+([0-9\.]+)", 1, Message)
| extend BreakerType = extract(@"\[([a-z_]+)\]", 1, Message)
| extend MemoryUsed = extract(@"(\d+)/(\d+)\s+bytes", 1, Message)
| summarize 
    CircuitBreakerTrips = count(),
    UniqueNodes = dcount(Computer),
    BreakerTypes = make_set(BreakerType)
    by SourceIP, bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)
| where CircuitBreakerTrips >= circuitBreakerThreshold
| project TimeGenerated, SourceIP, CircuitBreakerTrips, UniqueNodes, BreakerTypes
| order by CircuitBreakerTrips desc

index=elasticsearch sourcetype=elasticsearch:cluster
("circuit_breaking_exception" OR "OutOfMemoryError" OR "Data too large")
| rex field=_raw "from\s+(?<source_ip>[0-9\.]+)"
| rex field=_raw "\[(?<breaker_type>[a-z_]+)\]"
| bin _time span=1m
| stats count as breaker_trips, 
        dc(host) as affected_nodes,
        values(breaker_type) as breaker_types
        by _time, source_ip
| where breaker_trips > 5
| eval severity = case(
    breaker_trips > 20, "critical",
    breaker_trips > 10, "high",
    1=1, "medium"
  )
| sort -breaker_trips
| table _time, source_ip, breaker_trips, affected_nodes, breaker_types, severity

event.dataset: "elasticsearch.server" AND 
(
  message: "circuit_breaking_exception" OR 
  message: "Data too large" OR
  message: "OutOfMemoryError"
) AND 
event.category: "database"
| stats count() as trips, 
        cardinality(host.name) as node_count,
        values(elasticsearch.breaker.type) as breaker_types
        by source.ip, 
           event.created
| where trips > 5
| sort trips desc

# For AWS Elasticsearch Service
fields @timestamp, @message, sourceIpAddress
| filter @message like /circuit_breaking_exception|Data too large/
| stats count() as CircuitBreakerTrips by sourceIpAddress, bin(5m)
| filter CircuitBreakerTrips > 5
| sort CircuitBreakerTrips desc

tools.name: Elasticsearch Performance Analyzer
tools.usage: Provides real-time performance metrics and query profiling for OpenSearch/Elasticsearch

tools.name: Cerebro
tools.usage: Monitor cluster health, node stats, and identify problematic queries

compliance: PCI-DSS 3.2.1 (Requirement 6.5.10 - Broken Authentication and Session Management)
deployment: Cloud (AWS Elasticsearch, Azure Elasticsearch, Elastic Cloud)
